141	class SglFunction:
142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)
159	
160	    def run(
161	        self,
162	        *args,
163	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
164	        n: int = 1,
165	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
166	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
167	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
168	        temperature: float = 1.0,
169	        top_p: float = 1.0,
170	        top_k: int = -1,
171	        min_p: float = 0.0,
172	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
173	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
174	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
175	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
176	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
177	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
178	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
179	        stream: bool = False,
180	        backend=None,
181	        use_thread: bool = True,
182	        **kwargs,
183	    ):
184	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program
185	
186	        # avoid using [] as the default arg: https://nikos7am.com/posts/mutable-default-arguments/
187	        if stop is None:
188	            stop = []
189	        if stop_token_ids is None:
190	            stop_token_ids = []
191	        if stop_regex is None:
192	            stop_regex = []
193	
194	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
195	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
196	            n=n,
197	            stop=stop,
198	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
199	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
200	            temperature=temperature,
201	            top_p=top_p,
202	            top_k=top_k,
203	            min_p=min_p,
204	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
205	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
206	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
207	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
208	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
209	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
210	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
211	        )
212	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
213	        return run_program(
214	            self,
215	            backend,
216	            args,
217	            kwargs,
218	            default_sampling_para,
219	            stream,
220	            use_thread=use_thread,
221	        )
222	
223	    def run_batch(
224	        self,
225	        batch_kwargs,
226	        *,
227	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
228	        n: int = 1,
229	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
230	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
231	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
232	        temperature: float = 1.0,
233	        top_p: float = 1.0,
234	        top_k: int = -1,
235	        min_p: float = 0.0,
236	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
237	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
238	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
239	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
240	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
241	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
242	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
243	        backend=None,
244	        num_threads: Union[str, int] = "auto",
245	        progress_bar: bool = False,
246	        generator_style: bool = False,
247	    ):
248	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program_batch
249	
250	        if stop is None:
251	            stop = []
252	        if stop_token_ids is None:
253	            stop_token_ids = []
254	        if stop_regex is None:
255	            stop_regex = []
256	
257	        assert isinstance(batch_kwargs, (list, tuple))
258	        if len(batch_kwargs) == 0:
259	            return []
260	        if not isinstance(batch_kwargs[0], dict):
261	            num_programs = len(batch_kwargs)
262	            # change the list of argument values to dict of arg_name -> arg_value
263	            batch_kwargs = [
264	                {self.arg_names[i]: v for i, v in enumerate(arg_values)}
265	                for arg_values in batch_kwargs
266	                if isinstance(arg_values, (list, tuple))
267	                and len(self.arg_names) - len(self.arg_defaults)
268	                <= len(arg_values)
269	                <= len(self.arg_names)
270	            ]
271	            # Ensure to raise an exception if the number of arguments mismatch
272	            if len(batch_kwargs) != num_programs:
273	                raise Exception("Given arguments mismatch the SGL function signature")
274	
275	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
276	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
277	            n=n,
278	            stop=stop,
279	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
280	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
281	            temperature=temperature,
282	            top_p=top_p,
283	            top_k=top_k,
284	            min_p=min_p,
285	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
286	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
287	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
288	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
289	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
290	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
291	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
292	        )
293	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
294	        return run_program_batch(
295	            self,
296	            backend,
297	            batch_kwargs,
298	            default_sampling_para,
299	            num_threads,
300	            progress_bar,
301	            generator_style=generator_style,
302	        )
303	
304	    def trace(self, *, backend=None, **kwargs):
305	        from sglang.lang.tracer import trace_program
306	
307	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
308	        return trace_program(self, kwargs, backend)
309	
310	    def cache(self, backend=None):
311	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import cache_program
312	
313	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
314	        return cache_program(self, backend)
315	
316	    def __call__(self, *args, **kwargs):
317	        from sglang.lang.tracer import TracingScope
318	
319	        tracing_scope = TracingScope.get_current_scope()
320	        if tracing_scope is None:
321	            return self.run(*args, **kwargs)
322	        else:
323	            kwargs["backend"] = tracing_scope.tracer_state.backend
324	            return self.trace(*args, **kwargs)

327	class SglExpr:
328	    node_ct = 0
329	
330	    def __init__(self):
331	        self.node_id = SglExpr.node_ct
332	        self.prev_node = None
333	        self.pid = None
334	        SglExpr.node_ct += 1
335	
336	    def __add__(self, other):
337	        if isinstance(other, str):
338	            other = SglConstantText(other)
339	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr)
340	
341	        return self.concatenate_ir(self, other)
342	
343	    def __radd__(self, other):
344	        if isinstance(other, str):
345	            other = SglConstantText(other)
346	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr), f"{other}"
347	
348	        return self.concatenate_ir(other, self)
349	
350	    def concatenate_ir(self, a, b):
351	        if isinstance(a, SglExprList):
352	            if isinstance(b, SglExprList):
353	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + b.expr_list)
354	            else:
355	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + [b])
356	        elif isinstance(b, SglExprList):
357	            return SglExprList([a] + b.expr_list)
358	
359	        return SglExprList([a, b])
360	
361	    def print_graph_dfs(self):
362	        ret = [""]
363	        visited = set()
364	
365	        def dfs_print(x):
366	            if x is None or x in visited:
367	                return
368	            visited.add(x)
369	
370	            # Print dependency
371	            if x.prev_node is not None:
372	                dfs_print(x.prev_node)
373	
374	            if isinstance(x, SglExprList):
375	                for y in x.expr_list:
376	                    dfs_print(y)
377	            # elif isinstance(x, SglRole):
378	            #    dfs_print(x.expr)
379	            elif isinstance(x, SglVariable):
380	                dfs_print(x.source)
381	
382	            # Print the node itself
383	            if isinstance(x, (SglFork, SglGetForkItem)):
384	                ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = {x}\n"
385	            else:
386	                if x.prev_node is not None:
387	                    ret[0] += (
388	                        f"%{x.node_id} = %{x.prev_node.node_id} + " + str(x) + "\n"
389	                    )
390	                else:
391	                    ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = " + str(x) + "\n"
392	
393	        dfs_print(self)
394	        return ret[0]
395

619	class SglSeparateReasoning(SglExpr):
620	    def __init__(self, model_type: str, expr: SglExpr):
621	        super().__init__()
622	        self.model_type = model_type
623	
624	        self.expr = expr
625	        self.name = None
626	        self._process_expr(expr)
627	
628	    def process_name_for_reasoning(self, name):
629	        if not name:
630	            raise ValueError("name must be provided")
631	        return f"{name}_reasoning_content"
632	
633	    def _process_expr(self, expr):
634	        if isinstance(expr, SglGen):
635	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
636	        elif isinstance(expr, SglSelect):
637	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
638	        elif isinstance(expr, SglExprList):
639	            for x in expr.expr_list:
640	                self._process_expr(x)
641	
642	    def __repr__(self):
643	        return f"SeparateReasoning(model_type={self.model_type}, name={self.name})"

30	@dataclass
31	class GrammarStats:
32	    compilation_time: Optional[float] = None
33	    schema_count: Optional[int] = None
34	    ebnf_size: Optional[int] = None
35	    is_cache_hit: bool = False
36	    is_grammar_aborted: bool = False

42	class BaseGrammarObject:
43	
44	    def __init__(self):
45	        self._finished = False
46	        self.grammar_stats = None
47	        self.current_token = None
48	
49	    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
50	        pass
51	
52	    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
53	        """
54	        Accept a token in the grammar.
55	        """
56	        raise NotImplementedError()
57	
58	    def rollback(self, k: int):
59	        raise NotImplementedError()
60	
61	    def is_terminated(self):
62	        return False
63	
64	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
65	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
66	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
67	        raise NotImplementedError()
68	
69	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
70	        raise NotImplementedError()
71	
72	    @staticmethod
73	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
74	        raise NotImplementedError()
75	
76	    @staticmethod
77	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
78	        raise NotImplementedError()
79	
80	    def copy(self) -> "BaseGrammarObject":
81	        return self
82	
83	    @property
84	    def finished(self):
85	        return self._finished
86	
87	    @finished.setter
88	    def finished(self, finished):
89	        self._finished = finished
90	
91	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
92	        """
93	        Try to jump forward in the grammar.
94	
95	        Returns:
96	            A jump forward helper which may be used in `jump_forward_str_state`.
97	            None if the jump forward is not possible.
98	        """
99	        raise NotImplementedError()
100	
101	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
102	        """
103	        Jump forward for the grammar.
104	
105	        Returns:
106	            A tuple of the jump forward string and the next state of the grammar
107	            (which can be used in `jump_and_retokenize` if needed).
108	        """
109	        raise NotImplementedError()
110	
111	    def jump_and_retokenize(
112	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
113	    ) -> None:
114	        """
115	        Jump forward occurs, and update the grammar state if needed.
116	        """
117	        raise NotImplementedError()

120	INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ = BaseGrammarObject()
121	
122	
123	@dataclass
124	class CacheEntry:
125	    value: BaseGrammarObject
126	    event: Event
127	
128	
129	class BaseGrammarBackend:
130	    def __init__(self):
131	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
132	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
133	
134	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
135	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
136	
137	    def dispatch_fallback(
138	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
139	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
140	        """
141	        This function should not be reached in any case.
142	        """
143	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()
197	
198	
199	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}
200	
201	
202	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
203	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
204	
205	
206	def create_grammar_backend(
207	    server_args: ServerArgs,
208	    tokenizer,
209	    vocab_size: int,
210	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
211	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
212	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
213	
214	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
215	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
216	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
217	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
218	        )
219	
220	    # Default grammar backends
221	    if name == "outlines":
222	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
223	
224	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
225	            tokenizer,
226	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
227	        )
228	    elif name == "xgrammar":
229	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
230	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
231	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
232	        )
233	
234	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
235	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
236	
237	        try:
238	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
239	                tokenizer,
240	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
241	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
242	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
243	            )
244	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
245	            logger.warning(
246	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
247	                "Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
248	                "Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
249	            )
250	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
251	            return None
252	    elif name == "llguidance":
253	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
254	
255	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
256	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
257	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
258	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
259	        )
260	    elif name == "none":
261	        return None
262	    else:
263	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
264	
265	    if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
266	        from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
267	            ReasonerGrammarBackend,
268	        )
269	
270	        grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
271	            grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
272	        )
273	
274	    return grammar_backend
275

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import logging
4	import time
5	from concurrent import futures
6	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List
7	
8	import torch
9	
10	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
11	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
12	    create_grammar_backend,
13	)
14	from sglang.srt.environ import envs
15	
16	if TYPE_CHECKING:
17	    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq
18	    from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import Req
19	    from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import Scheduler
20	
21	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
22	
23	
24	class GrammarManager:
25	    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
26	        self.scheduler = scheduler
27	        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
28	        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
29	        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
30	            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
31	                self.server_args,
32	                scheduler.tokenizer,
33	                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
34	                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
35	            )
36	        else:
37	            self.grammar_backend = None
38	
39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
101	
102	        if add_to_grammar_queue:
103	            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
104	
105	        return add_to_grammar_queue
106	
107	    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
108	        """
109	        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.
110	
111	        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
112	        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
113	        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)
114	
115	        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
116	        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
117	        """
118	        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
119	        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
120	
121	        # Poll for ready requests
122	        start_time = time.perf_counter()
123	        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
124	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
125	                if i in ready_req_idxs:
126	                    continue
127	
128	                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
129	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
130	                    continue
131	
132	                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
133	                if req.grammar.done():
134	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
135	
136	            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
137	            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)
138	
139	        # Check failed requests
140	        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
141	            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
142	                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
143	                if (
144	                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
145	                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
146	                ):
147	                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
148	                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
149	                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)
150	
151	        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
152	        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
153	            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
154	            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
155	        else:
156	            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
157	            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
158	                all_gather_output,
159	                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
160	                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
161	            )
162	            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
163	            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])
164	
165	        # Return ready requests
166	        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
167	        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
168	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
169	            return_reqs.append(req)
170	            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
171	                continue
172	
173	            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
174	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
175	            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
176	                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
177	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
178	
179	        # Return failed requests
180	        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
181	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
182	            return_reqs.append(req)
183	
184	            req.grammar.cancel()
185	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
186	            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
187	            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
188	
189	        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
190	        self.grammar_queue = [
191	            req
192	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
193	            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
194	        ]
195	        return return_reqs
196

26	class RuntimeEndpoint(BaseBackend):
27	    def __init__(
28	        self,
29	        base_url: str,
30	        api_key: Optional[str] = None,
31	        verify: Optional[str] = None,
32	        chat_template_name: Optional[str] = None,
33	    ):
34	        super().__init__()
35	        self.support_concate_and_append = True
36	
37	        self.base_url = base_url
38	        self.api_key = api_key
39	        self.verify = verify
40	
41	        res = http_request(
42	            self.base_url + "/get_model_info",
43	            api_key=self.api_key,
44	            verify=self.verify,
45	        )
46	        self._assert_success(res)
47	        self.model_info = res.json()
48	
49	        if chat_template_name:
50	            self.chat_template = get_chat_template(chat_template_name)
51	        else:
52	            self.chat_template = get_chat_template_by_model_path(
53	                self.model_info["model_path"]
54	            )
55	
56	    def get_model_name(self):
57	        return self.model_info["model_path"]
58	
59	    def flush_cache(self):
60	        res = http_request(
61	            self.base_url + "/flush_cache",
62	            api_key=self.api_key,
63	            verify=self.verify,
64	            method="POST",
65	        )
66	        self._assert_success(res)
67

127	    def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
128	        if sampling_params.dtype is None:
129	            return
130	
131	        if sampling_params.stop == ():
132	            sampling_params.stop = []
133	
134	        dtype_regex = None
135	        if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:
136	
137	            dtype_regex = REGEX_INT
138	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
139	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:
140	
141	            dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT
142	            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
143	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:
144	
145	            dtype_regex = REGEX_STR
146	        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:
147	
148	            dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL
149	        else:
150	            raise RuntimeError(f"Invalid dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}")
151	
152	        if dtype_regex is not None and sampling_params.regex is not None:
153	            warnings.warn(
154	                f"Both dtype and regex are set. Only dtype will be used. dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}, regex: {sampling_params.regex}"
155	            )
156	
157	        sampling_params.regex = dtype_regex
158	
159	    def generate(
160	        self,
161	        s: StreamExecutor,
162	        sampling_params: SglSamplingParams,
163	    ):
164	        self._handle_dtype_to_regex(sampling_params)
165	        data = {
166	            "text": s.text_,
167	            "sampling_params": {
168	                "skip_special_tokens": global_config.skip_special_tokens_in_output,
169	                "spaces_between_special_tokens": global_config.spaces_between_special_tokens_in_out,
170	                **sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs(),
171	            },
172	        }
173	
174	        for item in [
175	            "return_logprob",
176	            "logprob_start_len",
177	            "top_logprobs_num",
178	            "return_text_in_logprobs",
179	        ]:
180	            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
181	            if value is not None:
182	                data[item] = value
183	
184	        self._add_images(s, data)
185	
186	        res = http_request(
187	            self.base_url + "/generate",
188	            json=data,
189	            api_key=self.api_key,
190	            verify=self.verify,
191	        )
192	        self._assert_success(res)
193	
194	        obj = res.json()
195	        comp = obj["text"]
196	        return comp, obj["meta_info"]
197	
198	    def generate_stream(
199	        self,
200	        s: StreamExecutor,
201	        sampling_params: SglSamplingParams,
202	    ):
203	        self._handle_dtype_to_regex(sampling_params)
204	
205	        data = {
206	            "text": s.text_,
207	            "sampling_params": {
208	                "skip_special_tokens": global_config.skip_special_tokens_in_output,
209	                "spaces_between_special_tokens": global_config.spaces_between_special_tokens_in_out,
210	                **sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs(),
211	            },
212	        }
213	
214	        for item in [
215	            "return_logprob",
216	            "logprob_start_len",
217	            "top_logprobs_num",
218	            "return_text_in_logprobs",
219	        ]:
220	            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
221	            if value is not None:
222	                data[item] = value
223	
224	        data["stream"] = True
225	        self._add_images(s, data)
226	
227	        res = http_request(
228	            self.base_url + "/generate",
229	            json=data,
230	            stream=True,
231	            api_key=self.api_key,
232	            verify=self.verify,
233	        )
234	        self._assert_success(res)
235	        pos = 0
236	
237	        for chunk in res.iter_lines(decode_unicode=False):
238	            chunk = chunk.decode("utf-8")
239	            if chunk and chunk.startswith("data:"):
240	                if chunk == "data: [DONE]":
241	                    break
242	                data = json.loads(chunk[5:].strip("\n"))
243	                chunk_text = data["text"][pos:]
244	                meta_info = data["meta_info"]
245	                pos += len(chunk_text)
246	                yield chunk_text, meta_info
247	
248	    def select(
249	        self,
250	        s: StreamExecutor,
251	        choices: List[str],
252	        temperature: float,
253	        choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod,
254	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
255	        assert temperature <= 1e-5
256	
257	        # Cache common prefix
258	        data = {"text": s.text_, "sampling_params": {"max_new_tokens": 0}}
259	        obj = self._generate_http_request(s, data)
260	        prompt_len = obj["meta_info"]["prompt_tokens"]
261	        logprob_start_len = max(prompt_len - 2, 0)  # For token healing
262	
263	        # Compute logprob
264	        data = {
265	            "text": [s.text_ + c for c in choices],
266	            "sampling_params": {
267	                "max_new_tokens": 0,
268	                "temperature": 0,
269	            },
270	            "return_logprob": True,
271	            "return_text_in_logprobs": True,
272	            "logprob_start_len": logprob_start_len,
273	        }
274	        obj = self._generate_http_request(s, data)
275	
276	        input_token_logprobs = [r["meta_info"]["input_token_logprobs"] for r in obj]
277	        output_token_logprobs = [r["meta_info"]["output_token_logprobs"] for r in obj]
278	        normalized_prompt_logprobs = [
279	            compute_normalized_prompt_logprobs(r["meta_info"]["input_token_logprobs"])
280	            for r in obj
281	        ]
282	
283	        # Remove extra token if no token healing occurred
284	        for i in range(len(input_token_logprobs)):
285	            healed_token_str = input_token_logprobs[i][0][-1]
286	            if s.text_.endswith(healed_token_str):
287	                healed_token_logprob = input_token_logprobs[i][0][0]
288	                normalized_prompt_logprobs[i] = (
289	                    normalized_prompt_logprobs[i] * len(input_token_logprobs[i])
290	                    - healed_token_logprob
291	                ) / (len(input_token_logprobs[i]) - 1)
292	                input_token_logprobs[i] = input_token_logprobs[i][1:]
293	
294	        # Compute unconditional logprobs if required
295	        if choices_method.requires_unconditional_logprobs:
296	            input_ids = [[el[1] for el in subl] for subl in input_token_logprobs]
297	            data = {
298	                "input_ids": input_ids,
299	                "sampling_params": {"max_new_tokens": 0},
300	                "return_logprob": True,
301	            }
302	            obj = self._generate_http_request(s, data)
303	            unconditional_token_logprobs = [
304	                r["meta_info"]["input_token_logprobs"] for r in obj
305	            ]
306	        else:
307	            unconditional_token_logprobs = None
308	
309	        return choices_method(
310	            choices=choices,
311	            normalized_prompt_logprobs=normalized_prompt_logprobs,
312	            input_token_logprobs=input_token_logprobs,
313	            output_token_logprobs=output_token_logprobs,
314	            unconditional_token_logprobs=unconditional_token_logprobs,
315	        )
316

337	    def _add_images(self, s: StreamExecutor, data):

342	    def _assert_success(self, res):
343	        if res.status_code != 200:
344	            try:
345	                content = res.json()
346	            except json.JSONDecodeError:
347	                content = res.text
348	            raise RuntimeError(content)

350	
351	def compute_normalized_prompt_logprobs(input_logprobs):
352	    values = [x[0] for x in input_logprobs if x[0]]
353	    return sum(values) / len(values)

356	class Runtime:
357	    """
358	    A wrapper for the HTTP server.
359	    This is used for launching the server in a python program without
360	    using the command line interface.
361	
362	    It is mainly used for the frontend language.
363	    You should use the Engine class if you want to do normal offline processing without the frontend language.
364	    """
365	
366	    def __init__(
367	        self,
368	        log_level: str = "error",
369	        launch_timeout: float = 300.0,
370	        *args,
371	        **kwargs,
372	    ):
373	        """See the arguments in server_args.py::ServerArgs
374	
375	        Args:
376	            log_level: Log level for the server.
377	            timeout: Timeout in seconds for waiting for the server to start.
378	            *args: Additional arguments passed to ServerArgs.
379	            **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments passed to ServerArgs.
380	        """
381	        # We delay the import of any `sglang.srt` components in `sglang.lang`, so users can run
382	        # client code without installing SRT server and its dependency if they want.
383	        from sglang.srt.entrypoints.http_server import launch_server
384	        from sglang.srt.server_args import ServerArgs
385	        from sglang.srt.utils import is_port_available
386	
387	        self.server_args = ServerArgs(*args, log_level=log_level, **kwargs)
388	
389	        # Pre-allocate ports
390	        for port in range(self.server_args.port, 40000):
391	            if is_port_available(port):
392	                break
393	        self.server_args.port = port
394	
395	        self.url = self.server_args.url()
396	        self.generate_url = self.url + "/generate"
397	
398	        # NOTE: We store pid instead of proc to fix some issues during __delete__
399	        self.pid = None
400	
401	        ctx = multiprocessing.get_context("spawn")
402	        proc = ctx.Process(
403	            target=launch_server,
404	            args=(self.server_args,),
405	        )
406	        proc.start()
407	        self.pid = proc.pid
408	
409	        # Before python program terminates, call shutdown implicitly. Therefore, users don't have to explicitly call .shutdown()
410	        atexit.register(self.shutdown)
411	
412	        # Wait for server to be ready by polling /health_generate
413	        start_time = time.time()
414	        with requests.Session() as session:
415	            while time.time() - start_time < launch_timeout:
416	                try:
417	                    response = session.get(f"{self.url}/health_generate")
418	                    if response.status_code == 200:
419	                        break
420	                except requests.RequestException:
421	                    pass
422	
423	                if not proc.is_alive():
424	                    self.shutdown()
425	                    raise RuntimeError(
426	                        "Initialization failed. Please see the error messages above."
427	                    )
428	
429	                time.sleep(2)
430	            else:
431	                self.shutdown()
432	                raise TimeoutError("Server failed to start within the timeout period.")
433	
434	        self.endpoint = RuntimeEndpoint(self.url)
435	
436	    def shutdown(self):
437	        from sglang.srt.utils import kill_process_tree
438	
439	        if self.pid is not None:
440	            kill_process_tree(self.pid)
441	            self.pid = None
442	
443	    def start_profile(self):
444	        self.endpoint.start_profile()
445	
446	    def stop_profile(self):
447	        self.endpoint.stop_profile()
448	
449	    def cache_prefix(self, prefix: str):
450	        self.endpoint.cache_prefix(prefix)
451	
452	    def get_tokenizer(self):
453	        from sglang.srt.utils.hf_transformers_utils import get_tokenizer
454	
455	        return get_tokenizer(
456	            self.server_args.tokenizer_path,
457	            tokenizer_mode=self.server_args.tokenizer_mode,
458	            trust_remote_code=self.server_args.trust_remote_code,
459	            revision=self.server_args.revision,
460	        )
461	
462	    async def async_generate(
463	        self,
464	        prompt: str,
465	        sampling_params: Optional[Dict] = None,
466	    ):
467	        if self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
468	            json_data = {
469	                "input_ids": prompt,
470	                "sampling_params": sampling_params,
471	                "stream": True,
472	            }
473	        else:
474	            json_data = {
475	                "text": prompt,
476	                "sampling_params": sampling_params,
477	                "stream": True,
478	            }
479	        pos = 0
480	
481	        timeout = aiohttp.ClientTimeout(total=3 * 3600)
482	        async with aiohttp.ClientSession(timeout=timeout, trust_env=True) as session:
483	            async with session.post(self.generate_url, json=json_data) as response:
484	                async for chunk, _ in response.content.iter_chunks():
485	                    chunk = chunk.decode("utf-8")
486	                    if chunk and chunk.startswith("data:"):
487	                        if chunk == "data: [DONE]\n\n":
488	                            break
489	                        data = json.loads(chunk[5:].strip("\n"))
490	                        if "text" in data:
491	                            cur = data["text"][pos:]
492	                            if cur:
493	                                yield cur
494	                            pos += len(cur)
495	                        else:
496	                            yield data
497	
498	    add_request = async_generate
499	
500	    def generate(
501	        self,
502	        prompt: Union[str, List[str]],
503	        sampling_params: Optional[Dict] = None,
504	        return_logprob: Optional[Union[List[bool], bool]] = False,
505	        logprob_start_len: Optional[Union[List[int], int]] = None,
506	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[Union[List[int], int]] = None,
507	        lora_path: Optional[List[Optional[str]]] = None,
508	    ):
509	        json_data = {
510	            "text": prompt,
511	            "sampling_params": sampling_params,
512	            "return_logprob": return_logprob,
513	            "logprob_start_len": logprob_start_len,
514	            "top_logprobs_num": top_logprobs_num,
515	            "lora_path": lora_path,
516	        }
517	        assert not isinstance(lora_path, list) or len(lora_path) == len(prompt)
518	        response = requests.post(
519	            self.url + "/generate",
520	            json=json_data,
521	        )
522	        return json.dumps(response.json())
523	
524	    def encode(
525	        self,
526	        prompt: Union[str, List[str], List[Dict], List[List[Dict]]],
527	    ):
528	        json_data = {"text": prompt}
529	        response = requests.post(self.url + "/encode", json=json_data)
530	        return json.dumps(response.json())
531	
532	    async def get_server_info(self):
533	        async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
534	            async with session.get(f"{self.url}/get_server_info") as response:
535	                if response.status == 200:
536	                    return await response.json()
537	                else:
538	                    error_data = await response.json()
539	                    raise RuntimeError(
540	                        f"Failed to get server info. {error_data['error']['message']}"
541	                    )
542	
543	    def __del__(self):
544	        self.shutdown()

20	class TestJSONModeMixin:
21	    """Mixin class containing JSON mode test methods"""
22	
23	    def test_json_mode_response(self):
24	        """Test that response_format json_object (also known as "json mode") produces valid JSON, even without a system prompt that mentions JSON."""
25	        response = self.client.chat.completions.create(
26	            model=self.model,
27	            messages=[
28	                # We are deliberately omitting "That produces JSON" or similar phrases from the assistant prompt so that we don't have misleading test results
29	                {
30	                    "role": "system",
31	                    "content": "You are a helpful AI assistant that gives a short answer.",
32	                },
33	                {"role": "user", "content": "What is the capital of Bulgaria?"},
34	            ],
35	            temperature=0,
36	            max_tokens=128,
37	            response_format={"type": "json_object"},
38	        )
39	        text = response.choices[0].message.content
40	
41	        print(f"Response ({len(text)} characters): {text}")
42	
43	        # Verify the response is valid JSON
44	        try:
45	            js_obj = json.loads(text)
46	        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
47	            self.fail(f"Response is not valid JSON. Error: {e}. Response: {text}")
48	
49	        # Verify it's actually an object (dict)
50	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj, dict, f"Response is not a JSON object: {text}")
51	
52	    def test_json_mode_with_streaming(self):
53	        """Test that streaming with json_object response (also known as "json mode") format works correctly, even without a system prompt that mentions JSON."""
54	        stream = self.client.chat.completions.create(
55	            model=self.model,
56	            messages=[
57	                # We are deliberately omitting "That produces JSON" or similar phrases from the assistant prompt so that we don't have misleading test results
58	                {
59	                    "role": "system",
60	                    "content": "You are a helpful AI assistant that gives a short answer.",
61	                },
62	                {"role": "user", "content": "What is the capital of Bulgaria?"},
63	            ],
64	            temperature=0,
65	            max_tokens=128,
66	            response_format={"type": "json_object"},
67	            stream=True,
68	        )
69	
70	        # Collect all chunks
71	        chunks = []
72	        for chunk in stream:
73	            if chunk.choices[0].delta.content is not None:
74	                chunks.append(chunk.choices[0].delta.content)
75	        full_response = "".join(chunks)
76	
77	        print(
78	            f"Concatenated Response ({len(full_response)} characters): {full_response}"
79	        )

8	class TestJSONConstrainedMixin:
9	    json_schema = json.dumps(
10	        {
11	            "type": "object",
12	            "properties": {
13	                "name": {"type": "string", "pattern": "^[\\w]+$"},
14	                "population": {"type": "integer"},
15	            },
16	            "required": ["name", "population"],
17	            "additionalProperties": False,
18	        }
19	    )
20	
21	    def _run_decode_json(
22	        self, json_schema, return_logprob=False, top_logprobs_num=0, n=1
23	    ):
24	        response = requests.post(
25	            self.base_url + "/generate",
26	            json={
27	                "text": (
28	                    "Introduce the capital of France. Return in a JSON format. The JSON Schema is: "
29	                    + json.dumps(json_schema)
30	                ),
31	                "sampling_params": {
32	                    "temperature": 0 if n == 1 else 0.5,
33	                    "max_new_tokens": 128,
34	                    "n": n,
35	                    "stop_token_ids": [119690],
36	                    "json_schema": json_schema,
37	                },
38	                "stream": False,
39	                "return_logprob": return_logprob,
40	                "top_logprobs_num": top_logprobs_num,
41	                "logprob_start_len": 0,
42	            },
43	        )
44	        ret = response.json()
45	        print(json.dumps(ret))
46	        print("=" * 100)
47	
48	        if not json_schema or json_schema == "INVALID":
49	            return
50	
51	        # Make sure the json output is valid
52	        try:
53	            js_obj = json.loads(ret["text"])
54	        except (TypeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError):
55	            raise
56	
57	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["name"], str)
58	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["population"], int)

60	    def test_json_generate(self):
61	        self._run_decode_json(json_schema=self.json_schema)

64	        self._run_decode_json(json_schema="INVALID")
65

84	            },

87	
88	        try:

95	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["population"], int)

97	    def test_mix_json_and_other(self):

99

1	# Sampling Parameters
2	
3	This doc describes the sampling parameters of the SGLang Runtime. It is the low-level endpoint of the runtime.
4	If you want a high-level endpoint that can automatically handle chat templates, consider using the [OpenAI Compatible API](openai_api_completions.ipynb).
5	
6	## `/generate` Endpoint
7	
8	The `/generate` endpoint accepts the following parameters in JSON format. For detailed usage, see the [native API doc](native_api.ipynb). The object is defined at `io_struct.py::GenerateReqInput`. You can also read the source code to find more arguments and docs.
9	
10	| Argument                   | Type/Default                                                                 | Description                                                                                                                                                     |
11	|----------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
12	| text                       | `Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None`                                     | The input prompt. Can be a single prompt or a batch of prompts.                                                                                                 |
13	| input_ids                  | `Optional[Union[List[List[int]], List[int]]] = None`                         | The token IDs for text; one can specify either text or input_ids.                                                                                               |
14	| input_embeds               | `Optional[Union[List[List[List[float]]], List[List[float]]]] = None`         | The embeddings for input_ids; one can specify either text, input_ids, or input_embeds.                                                                          |
15	| image_data                 | `Optional[Union[List[List[ImageDataItem]], List[ImageDataItem], ImageDataItem]] = None` | The image input. Supports three formats: (1) **Raw images**: PIL Image, file path, URL, or base64 string; (2) **Processor output**: Dict with `format: "processor_output"` containing HuggingFace processor outputs; (3) **Precomputed embeddings**: Dict with `format: "precomputed_embedding"` and `feature` containing pre-calculated visual embeddings. Can be a single image, list of images, or list of lists of images. See [Multimodal Input Formats](#multimodal-input-formats) for details. |
16	| audio_data                 | `Optional[Union[List[AudioDataItem], AudioDataItem]] = None`                 | The audio input. Can be a file name, URL, or base64 encoded string.                                                                                             |
17	| sampling_params            | `Optional[Union[List[Dict], Dict]] = None`                                   | The sampling parameters as described in the sections below.                                                                                                     |
18	| rid                        | `Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None`                                     | The request ID.                                                                                                                                                 |
19	| return_logprob             | `Optional[Union[List[bool], bool]] = None`                                   | Whether to return log probabilities for tokens.                                                                                                                 |
20	| logprob_start_len          | `Optional[Union[List[int], int]] = None`                                     | If return_logprob, the start location in the prompt for returning logprobs. Default is "-1", which returns logprobs for output tokens only.                     |
21	| top_logprobs_num           | `Optional[Union[List[int], int]] = None`                                     | If return_logprob, the number of top logprobs to return at each position.                                                                                       |
22	| token_ids_logprob          | `Optional[Union[List[List[int]], List[int]]] = None`                         | If return_logprob, the token IDs to return logprob for.                                                                                                         |
23	| return_text_in_logprobs    | `bool = False`                                                               | Whether to detokenize tokens in text in the returned logprobs.                                                                                                  |
24	| stream                     | `bool = False`                                                               | Whether to stream output.                                                                                                                                       |
25	| lora_path                  | `Optional[Union[List[Optional[str]], Optional[str]]] = None`                 | The path to the LoRA.                                                                                                                                           |
26	| custom_logit_processor     | `Optional[Union[List[Optional[str]], str]] = None`                           | Custom logit processor for advanced sampling control. Must be a serialized instance of `CustomLogitProcessor` using its `to_str()` method. For usage see below. |
27	| return_hidden_states       | `Union[List[bool], bool] = False`                                            | Whether to return hidden states.                                                                                                                                |
28	| return_routed_experts      | `bool = False`                                                               | Whether to return routed experts for MoE models. Requires `--enable-return-routed-experts` server flag. Returns base64-encoded int32 expert IDs as a flattened array with logical shape `[num_tokens, num_layers, top_k]`. |
29	
30	## Sampling parameters
31	
32	The object is defined at `sampling_params.py::SamplingParams`. You can also read the source code to find more arguments and docs.
33	
34	### Note on defaults
35	
36	By default, SGLang initializes several sampling parameters from the model's `generation_config.json` (when the server is launched with `--sampling-defaults model`, which is the default). To use SGLang/OpenAI constant defaults instead, start the server with `--sampling-defaults openai`. You can always override any parameter per request via `sampling_params`.
37	
38	```bash
39	# Use model-provided defaults from generation_config.json (default behavior)
40	python -m sglang.launch_server --model-path <MODEL> --sampling-defaults model
41	
42	# Use SGLang/OpenAI constant defaults instead
43	python -m sglang.launch_server --model-path <MODEL> --sampling-defaults openai
44	```
45	
46	### Core parameters
47	
48	| Argument        | Type/Default                                 | Description                                                                                                                                    |
49	|-----------------|----------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
50	| max_new_tokens  | `int = 128`                                  | The maximum output length measured in tokens.                                                                                                  |
51	| stop            | `Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None`     | One or multiple [stop words](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create#chat-create-stop). Generation will stop if one of these words is sampled. |
52	| stop_token_ids  | `Optional[List[int]] = None`                 | Provide stop words in the form of token IDs. Generation will stop if one of these token IDs is sampled.                                        |
53	| stop_regex      | `Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None`     | Stop when hitting any of the regex patterns in this list |
54	| temperature     | `float (model default; fallback 1.0)`        | [Temperature](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create#chat-create-temperature) when sampling the next token. `temperature = 0` corresponds to greedy sampling, a higher temperature leads to more diversity. |
55	| top_p           | `float (model default; fallback 1.0)`        | [Top-p](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create#chat-create-top_p) selects tokens from the smallest sorted set whose cumulative probability exceeds `top_p`. When `top_p = 1`, this reduces to unrestricted sampling from all tokens. |
56	| top_k           | `int (model default; fallback -1)`           | [Top-k](https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/how-to-get-better-outputs-from-your-large-language-model/#predictability_vs_creativity) randomly selects from the `k` highest-probability tokens. |
57	| min_p           | `float (model default; fallback 0.0)`        | [Min-p](https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/issues/27670) samples from tokens with probability larger than `min_p * highest_token_probability`. |
58	
59	### Penalizers
60

60	
61	| Argument           | Type/Default           | Description                                                                                                                                    |
62	|--------------------|------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
63	| frequency_penalty  | `float = 0.0`          | Penalizes tokens based on their frequency in generation so far. Must be between `-2` and `2` where negative numbers encourage repeatment of tokens and positive number encourages sampling of new tokens. The scaling of penalization grows linearly with each appearance of a token. |
64	| presence_penalty   | `float = 0.0`          | Penalizes tokens if they appeared in the generation so far. Must be between `-2` and `2` where negative numbers encourage repeatment of tokens and positive number encourages sampling of new tokens. The scaling of the penalization is constant if a token occurred. |
65	| repetition_penalty | `float = 1.0`          | Scales the logits of previously generated tokens to discourage (values > 1) or encourage (values < 1) repetition. Valid range is `[0, 2]`; `1.0` leaves probabilities unchanged. |
66	| min_new_tokens     | `int = 0`              | Forces the model to generate at least `min_new_tokens` until a stop word or EOS token is sampled. Note that this might lead to unintended behavior, for example, if the distribution is highly skewed towards these tokens. |
67	
68	### Constrained decoding
69	
70	Please refer to our dedicated guide on [constrained decoding](../advanced_features/structured_outputs.ipynb) for the following parameters.
71	
72	| Argument        | Type/Default                    | Description                                                                                                                                    |
73	|-----------------|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
74	| json_schema     | `Optional[str] = None`          | JSON schema for structured outputs.                                                                                                            |
75	| regex           | `Optional[str] = None`          | Regex for structured outputs.                                                                                                                  |
76	| ebnf            | `Optional[str] = None`          | EBNF for structured outputs.                                                                                                                   |
77	| structural_tag  | `Optional[str] = None`          | The structural tag for structured outputs.                                                                                                       |
78	
79	### Other options
80	
81	| Argument                      | Type/Default                    | Description                                                                                                                                    |
82	|-------------------------------|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
83	| n                             | `int = 1`                       | Specifies the number of output sequences to generate per request. (Generating multiple outputs in one request (n > 1) is discouraged; repeating the same prompts several times offers better control and efficiency.) |
84	| ignore_eos                    | `bool = False`                  | Don't stop generation when EOS token is sampled.                                                                                               |
85	| skip_special_tokens           | `bool = True`                   | Remove special tokens during decoding.                                                                                                         |
86	| spaces_between_special_tokens | `bool = True`                   | Whether or not to add spaces between special tokens during detokenization.                                                                     |
87	| no_stop_trim                  | `bool = False`                  | Don't trim stop words or EOS token from the generated text.                                                                                    |
88	| custom_params                 | `Optional[List[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]]] = None` | Used when employing `CustomLogitProcessor`. For usage, see below.                                                                              |
89	
90	## Examples
91	
92	### Normal
93	
94	Launch a server:
95	
96	```bash
97	python -m sglang.launch_server --model-path meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct --port 30000
98	```
99	
100	Send a request:
101	
102	```python
103	import requests
104	
105	response = requests.post(
106	    "http://localhost:30000/generate",
107	    json={
108	        "text": "The capital of France is",
109	        "sampling_params": {
110	            "temperature": 0,
111	            "max_new_tokens": 32,
112	        },
113	    },
114	)
115	print(response.json())
116	```
117	
118	Detailed example in [send request](./send_request.ipynb).
119
